skill.name: finishing-a-development-branch
skill.description: Guides a safe branch-completion workflow after implementation and tests are complete, including merge, pull request, keep, or discard choices.
skill.summary: Finish completed development branches with test checks, clear integration choices, and controlled cleanup.
skill.author: Cygnusfear

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many command-like patterns, but review found they are Markdown workflow examples for legitimate git, test, and PR operations. No prompt injection, malware, credential access, or network exfiltration evidence was found. The main confirmed risk is operational: the skill can guide destructive git cleanup commands, so publication should include a warning about user confirmation and repository backups.

medium_findings.title: Destructive Git Cleanup Commands
medium_findings.description: The workflow includes branch deletion and worktree removal commands. This is legitimate branch cleanup, but it can permanently remove local branch references or worktree files if used on the wrong target.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines directly show git branch deletion and worktree removal commands. The surrounding workflow requires confirmation, which lowers severity but does not remove the operational risk.

medium_findings.title: Local Command Execution Workflow
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to run project test commands, git commands, and GitHub CLI commands. These commands are expected for the workflow, but they execute local project tooling and can change repository state.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command blocks are explicit and semantically tied to completing a branch. Legitimate use is clear, but external command execution is still an elevated-risk capability.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Ruby Backtick Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported Ruby backtick execution across the Markdown file. Review found fenced bash snippets and plain text examples, not Ruby code or dynamic shell interpolation inside an executable script.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is SKILL.md and the detected locations are Markdown code fences. No executable Ruby source or backtick operator use was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged line 3 as weak cryptography. That line is only the skill description and contains no hash function, encryption function, or security-sensitive cryptographic operation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is YAML front matter text describing the skill. There is no cryptographic API or algorithm reference at that location.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Standard Device File Access
low_findings.description: Line 44 redirects git error output to /dev/null while probing common base branches. This is a normal shell redirection pattern and does not read sensitive files or exfiltrate data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only device-file reference is stderr redirection to /dev/null in a git merge-base command. The context is benign branch detection.

dangerous_patterns.title: Force Branch Deletion and Worktree Removal
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documents git branch -D and git worktree remove for discard or cleanup flows. These commands are powerful and should only run after verifying branch names, worktree paths, and user confirmation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicitly present and can delete local repository state. The workflow includes confirmation, so this is dangerous by capability rather than malicious intent.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Substitution in PR Body
dangerous_patterns.description: The PR creation example uses shell command substitution to build a pull request body. The heredoc content is templated and local, but command substitution should not include untrusted data.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command substitution is visible in the GitHub CLI example. The shown data is static template text, so injection risk is limited unless modified with untrusted content.

content.value_statement: Completed branches often stall when merge, pull request, and cleanup choices are unclear. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured finish workflow.
actual_capabilities: Verifies tests before presenting branch completion options.
Helps identify a likely base branch using git merge-base.
Presents four completion paths: merge, create PR, keep branch, or discard work.
Guides local merge steps with a second test check after merge.
Provides a pull request creation template using GitHub CLI.
Defines cleanup rules for branches and git worktrees.
limitations: Assumes the repository uses git and has a runnable test command.
Requires GitHub CLI only for the pull request path.
Does not resolve merge conflicts or failing tests by itself.
Destructive cleanup commands still require careful user confirmation.

use_cases.title: Complete a Feature Branch
use_cases.description: Run final checks, choose a merge or PR path, and clean up branch state after a feature is done.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Handoffs
use_cases.description: Give contributors the same end-of-work choices before code reaches main or a pull request.

use_cases.title: Manage Worktree Cleanup
use_cases.description: Remove temporary git worktrees only when the selected workflow makes cleanup appropriate.

prompt_templates.title: Finish My Branch
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner workflow after tests appear ready.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to finish my current development branch. Verify tests first, then present the available completion options.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a Pull Request
prompt_templates.scenario: Create a PR after implementation is complete.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to prepare a pull request for my current branch. Include a concise summary and a test plan.

prompt_templates.title: Merge Locally With Cleanup
prompt_templates.scenario: Integrate work into the base branch on the local machine.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to merge the completed branch locally. Confirm the base branch, run tests after merge, and clean up only when safe.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Branch Completion Choices
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review before deleting or preserving branch state.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review my branch completion options. Explain the repository state, risks, cleanup impact, and required confirmations before acting.

output_examples.input: The implementation is complete and tests pass. Finish this branch.
output: Tests passed and the branch is ready for a completion decision.
The assistant presents merge, pull request, keep, and discard options.
No branch deletion happens until the user chooses and confirms the relevant path.

output_examples.input: Create a pull request for this finished feature branch.
output: The branch is pushed to the remote repository.
A pull request is created with a summary and test plan.
The worktree is preserved because the PR path keeps review context available.
The assistant lists the branch, commits, and worktree that would be removed.
The user must type the required confirmation word.
Only then does the assistant delete the branch and remove the worktree.

best_practices: Run the real project test command before choosing a completion path.
Confirm branch names, base branch, and worktree paths before cleanup.
Prefer the pull request path when review or CI validation is required.
anti_patterns: Do not merge or create a pull request while tests are failing.
Do not delete a branch or worktree without explicit confirmation.
Do not force-push or rewrite branch history unless the user asks for it.

faq.question: What problem does this skill solve?
faq.answer: It turns the end of a coding branch into a clear sequence of tests, choices, action, and cleanup.

faq.question: Does it automatically merge code?
faq.answer: No. It first verifies tests and asks the user to choose one of four completion paths.

faq.question: Can it create pull requests?
faq.answer: Yes, when GitHub CLI is available and the user chooses the pull request option.

faq.question: Can it delete local work?
faq.answer: Yes, the discard and cleanup paths can delete branches or worktrees after confirmation.

faq.question: Does it support non-git repositories?
faq.answer: No. The workflow is written for git repositories and git worktrees.

faq.question: Is it safe for Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: It is suitable with warnings because it uses local git commands and includes destructive cleanup options.
